title: Athyrium koryoense
taxonomy: Athyrium koryoense is a species of the Athyrium genus, which is classified under the family Athyriaceae. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns. The order Polypodiales is further classified under the subclass Polypodiidae, which is a part of the class Polypodiopsida, also known as the leptosporangiate ferns. The class Polypodiopsida is a subset of the division Pteridophyta, the ferns. The kingdom Plantae encompasses all the plant species, including ferns like Athyrium koryoense.
uses: Athyrium koryoense is a species of fern that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can be beneficial for treating various health conditions. In traditional medicine, Athyrium koryoense is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential in treating cancer and other diseases. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Athyrium koryoense as a medicinal plant.

intro: Athyrium koryoense is a species of fern known as Korean lady fern, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium koryoense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
